title: Garlicky Creamy Vietnamese Napa Cabbage Cha Gio
description: A creamy, savory, and nostalgic Vietnamese dish featuring napa cabbage, served with crispy toast and a side of fresh herbs.
instructions: Shred the napa cabbage and soak it in cold water for 30 minutes to remove excess moisture.
Drain the cabbage and squeeze it dry with paper towels.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat and saut5 the onion until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the mushroom and cook until tender, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the pork sausage and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the fish sauce, soy sauce, sugar, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the shredded cabbage and stir-fry until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Beat the egg in a small bowl and add it to the cabbage mixture, stirring to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a plate and let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, shape the mixture into small cylinders and coat with toasted breadcrumbs.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a pan over medium-high heat and fry the cha gio until crispy and golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Serve the cha gio with crispy toast, sliced chilies, and fresh cilantro on the side.
Garnish with additional cilantro if desired.
